instincts.trigger: e2e test XR never becomes Available in a Crossplane module test
instincts.action: Check the Helm Release status in kube-system for error messages. Common issues: missing forProvider.namespace, pull secret for private charts, or prerequisite CRDs/namespaces not created before the XR.

instincts.trigger: upgrading a Crossplane provider in a test or script environment
instincts.action: Patch spec.package in-place via r.Update(ctx, patch) instead of delete+recreate. Deleting a Provider wipes its CRDs which destroys all ProviderConfig and ClusterProviderConfig instances.

instincts.trigger: Helm chart pull fails with 401 from a private OCI registry during Crossplane composition
instincts.action: Add pullSecretRef to the Release base in the composition YAML. The referenced Secret must have username and password keys (not dockerconfigjson format). Create the Secret before the Release via a setup step.

instincts.trigger: Crossplane composition Helm Release fails with 'Namespace is invalid'
instincts.action: Add the namespace field to the Release's spec.forProvider in the composition YAML. Older provider-helm versions lack this field — upgrade to v1.0.2+ via spec.package patch.

instincts.trigger: writing Crossplane module e2e tests with sdk-module
instincts.action: Order setup steps: 1) XRDs, 2) Composition, 3) runtime config + RBAC + functions, 4) provider upgrade (if needed), 5) ProviderConfig, 6) pull secrets + namespaces, 7) XR. ProviderConfig must be applied AFTER provider upgrade to survive CRD recreation.

instincts.trigger: e2e test cleanup fails with 'ClusterProviderConfig not found'
instincts.action: This happens when a provider was deleted and recreated, wiping CRD instances. Switch to in-place spec.package update instead of delete+recreate to keep CRD instances intact.
